HEIZER MIDDLE SCHOOL is looking for a Football Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Roswell Independent Schools is looking for a Football Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Roswell Independent School District Job Description Job Title: ASSISTANT COACH Reports To: HEAD COACH/PRINCIPAL/DIRECTOR OF ATHLETICS/ACTIVITIES Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Has knowledge of existing system and State and NMAA regulations. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Roswell Independent School District Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation. Has knowledge of the existing system, state and league regulations and implements them consistently. Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperate within the school sports program. Recommends to the head coach budgetary items for next year in his area of the program. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches offices and authorizes who may enter. Is accountable to the head coach for all equipment. Collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned and arranges for issuing and storing of equipment and submits to the head coach an annual inventory and current records concerning this equipment. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of the specific equipment. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper usage. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Assists the head coach in scheduling, providing transportation to tournaments and special sporting events. Directs student managers and statisticians on respective games. Provides training rules and any other unique regulation of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Determines student discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary. By his presence at all practices, games and while traveling, provides assistance and guidance to and safeguards for each participant. Instructs team members as to changes in the rules and teaches fundamentals of the sport as outlined by the head coach. Assists the head coach in carrying out his responsibility. Attends all staff meetings and carries out scouting assignments as outlined by the head coach. Works within the basic framework and philosophy of the head coach of that sport. Arrives early enough before practice, contests and meetings to adequately prepare and remains long enough afterwards to help players with problems or to become involved in staff discussions. Never criticizes, admonishes or argues with head coach or any staff member within ears or eyes of players or parents. Helps in the planning and implementation of both in-season and out-or-season conditioning and weight programs as is possible. Attends most of the contests, when possible, or other teams in the program. Strives to improve skills by attending clinics and using resources made available by the head coach. Performs such other duties that are consistent with the nature of the position and that may be requested by the head coach. Maintain confidentiality with sensitive matters. Be flexible and able to prioritize tasks and maintain accurate and detailed records. Report to work on time and evening /weekend hours are applicable. Attempt to deescalate students/parents/staff; listening/visiting with them about their concerns prior to them meeting with administrators. May be required to perform other duties/functions as directed by supervisor. Page 1 of 2 Roswell Independent School District ASSISTANT COACH (CONT’D) Supervisory Responsibilities: Students in his charge. Qualifications: High School diploma or GED. Current NM Coaching licensure including CPR/AED Certification. Valid Driver’s license and Car Insurance. Ability to drive from site to site as needed.
... Find more information at: https://roswellnm.schoolspring.com/?jobid=5454559

Job.JobTitle: Coach Assistant Boys Basketball High School Posting 550
Job.JobDescription:
Los Alamos Public Schools is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Visa Sponsorship is not available Successful experience as either a head or assistant coach, or successful experience as a player or coach at the college level. To direct the planning, preparations and coaching activities of the boys basketball program from the mid-school level to the high school. To plan, schedule and conduct a regular program of practice and competition in accordance with rules of the school, the NMAA, and District 2-4A. To assist the Athletic Director and Athletic Trainer in the distribution and collection of appropriate information i.e. Physical forms, and all other forms that are required by the NMAA, District 2-4A and the high school. To attend the appropriate meetings pertaining to basketball as scheduled by the NMAA or District 2-4A. To recommend purchase of equipment, supplies and uniforms as needed. To assume responsibility for maintaining an accurate inventory of equipment pertaining to the sport. To oversee the safety and conditions of the facility or area assigned to the boys basketball programs. To assume responsibility for supervision, safety and welfare of students participating in the basketball program before, during and after scheduled practices and competitive contests. To establish and maintain good rapport with athletes, staff, parents, community groups and news media. To assume responsibility for submitting request for transportation forms to the Athletic Director. To assist the Athletic Director in planning and organization of promotion and participant recruitment and recognition programs pertaining to the sport. To perform assigned tasks in a timely and efficient manner. To perform assigned tasks with a high standard of quality. To perform other duties as assigned by the Principal or designee.

Job.JobDescription: Wappingers Central S.D. is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.

Wappingers Central S.D. is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
DISTINGUISHING FEATURES OF THE CLASS: Provides for the care, prevention and treatment of athletic injuries and/or improves physical fitness of athletes for participation in various sports programs. Under the general supervision of a licensed physician and the District Athletic Director, incumbents of this position are responsible for the care and prevention and treatment of athletic injuries involving student athletes. In accordance with Section 8532 of New York State Education Law the practice of the profession of athletic training is defined as the application of principles and methods of conditioning athletes, preventing and treating athletic injuries through the use of appropriate preventative and supportive devices, under the direction of a physician, recognizing and evaluating athletic injuries, administering first aid and emergency care, developing and implementing treatment and rehabilitation programs for athletic injuries under the direction of a physician and the planning and implementation of programs and services to help prevent athletic injuries. Incumbents are responsible for the record keeping of athletic injuries and treatments. Does related work as required.

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Coach - High School Boys Lacrosse
Job.JobDescription:
Jenks Public Schools is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Jenks Public Schools Job Description Position: Assistant Coach - High School Boys Lacrosse Reports to: Director of Community Education and Assistant Director – Athletics, Community Education Supervises: None Qualifications: High School diploma or GED required. Has previous success in assigned coaching position. Extensive knowledge, skills and ability in the strategy and technology of the sport. Experience interacting with student athletes, parents, and assistant coaches through deliberate positive interactions. Ability to serve as an effective role model for student athletes and represent Jenks Public Schools and Jenks Community Education with integrity and respect. Satisfy all district requirements with regard to physical health and citizenship. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board may find appropriate and acceptable. Position Goal: Assist with all aspects of student-athlete development and performance of the lacrosse program. Essential Functions and Responsibilities: Assist in the planning, development, and the execution of the high school boys lacrosse program by focusing on building a positive culture and developing quality relationships with student athletes and parents. Assist in the implementation of sound practices and skill development that maximizes student athlete performance in lacrosse in collaboration with the head coach. Communicate effectively with all program stakeholders to ensure a positive, quality experience is provided for our student athletes. Collaborate with the coordinator, assistant coordinator, and the Booster Club. Assist in providing direction and evaluation of youth coaches and teams to develop vertical alignment throughout the program. Employment Terms: Community Education Classified - Temporary Contract FLSA Qualification: Non-Exempt Evaluation: None

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Head Men's Volleyball Coach – Bend Senior High School – Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Bend Senior High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Description Bend-La Pine Schools is committed to the principle of equity. Equity supersedes the notion of equality, where all are treated the same. Pursuing equity requires the removal of barriers and the promotion of inclusive practices so that all students fully benefit. The principle of equity will inform all BLS policies, regulations, programs, operations, practices, and resource allocations. Studies have shown that some individuals (women and people of color, for example) are less likely to apply for jobs unless they believe they meet every single qualification in a job description. Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application. Classification Title: Boys' Volleyball Coach Reports To: Athletic Director Pay: Extra Duty Salary Schedule, Range Classification Definition: This classification encompasses assignments that support co-curricular and extracurricular programming across athletics, student activities, and enrichment opportunities. These roles provide students with structured opportunities that extend beyond the core curriculum and may occur outside the traditional classroom setting, and promote skill-building, personal development, and increased belonging. Assignments under this classification are made on a year-to-year basis and may be seasonal or year long, depending on program needs.

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Boy's Tennis Head Coach - Caldera High School - Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Caldera High School is looking for a Tennis Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application. As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit, any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement. Bend La Pine Schools Head Coaching Details Applicant must submit: 1. Letter of application detailing experience. 2. Current Resume – listing experience related to this position and working with students. 3. A minimum of three letters of recommendation. 4. A minimum of three references with contact information. General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Head Coach: Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability academically and behaviorallyfor all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for program. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Understand and provide information regarding eligibility, tryout process, cut process, and all other team-related information to include lettering, team rules, etc. Be creative and involved in all fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with ALL stakeholders. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete.
Job.Location: Caldera High School, 60925 SE 15th Street, Bend, OR, 97702, United States

Job.JobDescription:
JobID: 1153 is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: High School Assistant Coach Reports To: Director of Athletics & High School Head Coach Salary: Stipend POSITION GOAL: The successful candidate will be responsible for creating a safe and positive atmosphere that welcomes and respects all individuals while promoting and maintaining safe conditions in accordance with PIAA, league, sports medicine and RSD policies/procedures. Furthermore the candidate will be responsible for assisting student-athletes to establish a life after high school while instilling Red Knight pride, connecting with youth programs, teaching education through athletics, and fundraising. QUALIFICATIONS: Must be a minimum age of 18 years old. High School Diploma or equivalent required. Preferable experience working in a supervision role in athletics, recreation, education, or related fields. Exceptional interpersonal skills including the ability to effectively communicate with adults and students in a diverse work environment. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Supervise student athletes Stay current with current rules, coaching techniques, schemes, and best practices Monitor student-athletes to prevent accidents and injuries. Place student-athletes in positions to succeed not only in their sport, but also in life Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills including communication skills with sports medicine staff, game workers, officials, media, students, parents, spectators, and facility staff. Ensures that all possible safety measures are observed, including taking proper care of equipment and proper surveillance of program participants. Attend all training sessions and any additional staff meetings. Work flexible hours including off season training, early mornings, afternoon/evenings, weekends, and holidays. Verify student-athletes are eligible by PIAA and Reading School District Athletic policies. Secure equipment and facilities after each usage Communicate with RSD admin, safe schools, custodial services and/or RPD as needed. Communicate with youth groups and foster participation growth Be a good role model to student-athletes Support all RSD athletics PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Physically perform all skills required of a coach. Hear noises and distress signals in a crowded athletic facility. Able to withstand extreme weather conditions Able to lift at least 20 pounds.

Job.JobDescription:
ALCORN MIDDLE SCHOOL is looking for a Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Richland County School District One, South Carolina Classification Description Classification Title: Supplemental Coach Job Summary: We are seeking a dynamic, passionate, and skilled Supplemental Athletic Coach to assist in the development and coaching of student-athletes in various sports programs. This position will focus on enhancing individual and team performance, supporting the head coach, and promoting a positive and inclusive athletic environment. The Supplemental Athletic Coach will work closely with athletes, coaches, and staff to help guide students toward their full potential, both in sport and personal development. Key Responsibilities: · Assist the head coach with planning and implementing daily practice sessions, drills, and team strategies. · Provide individualized coaching and support to athletes to help improve their technical skills, athletic performance, and teamwork. · Foster a positive and motivating atmosphere that encourages the growth and development of all student-athletes. · Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback to improve skills, strength, and conditioning. · Support the organization and management of game-day operations, including warm-ups, player preparation, and team logistics. · Collaborate with coaching staff to set team goals and help track progress toward those goals. · Assist in the monitoring of athlete eligibility, attendance, and participation requirements. · Ensure that all practices and events comply with relevant regulations, rules, and school policies. · Help maintain and organize athletic equipment and ensure the safety of all participants during training and events. · Serve as a mentor and role model, promoting sportsmanship, leadership, and respect for others. · Communicate regularly with parents and guardians regarding athlete progress and team matters. · Contribute to the recruitment of new athletes and support the retention of current team members. Qualifications: · Previous coaching experience in the relevant sport (1-3 years preferred). · Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. · Ability to communicate effectively with athletes, parents, staff, and administration. · Strong organizational and time-management skills. · A commitment to promoting a positive and inclusive team environment. · CPR and First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain). · High school diploma or equivalent (Bachelor's degree in Physical Education, Sports Management, or a related field is a plus). · Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as needed. Desired Skills & Abilities: · Strong interpersonal and motivational skills to connect with athletes. · Ability to adapt coaching techniques to the needs and skill levels of individual athletes. · Experience with strength and conditioning, as well as injury prevention and rehabilitation. · Enthusiasm for teaching and mentoring young athletes. · Knowledge of current trends in sports coaching and athlete development. Physical Requirements: · Ability to participate in physical activities such as drills, conditioning, and demonstrations. · Ability to lift and carry athletic equipment and materials. · Ability to stand, walk, and move around frequently during practices and games. Working Conditions: · The position may require working outside in various weather conditions. · Some travel may be required for away games and tournaments.

Qualifications: · Previous coaching experience in the relevant sport (1-3 years preferred). · Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. · Ability to communicate effectively with athletes, parents, staff, and administration. · Strong organizational and time-management skills. · A commitment to promoting a positive and inclusive team environment. · CPR and First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain). · High school diploma or equivalent (Bachelor's degree in Physical Education, Sports Management, or a related field is a plus). · Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as needed. Desired Skills & Abilities: · Strong interpersonal and motivational skills to connect with athletes. · Ability to adapt coaching techniques to the needs and skill levels of individual athletes. · Experience with strength and conditioning, as well as injury prevention and rehabilitation. · Enthusiasm for teaching and mentoring young athletes. · Knowledge of current trends in sports coaching and athlete development. Physical Requirements: · Ability to participate in physical activities such as drills, conditioning, and demonstrations. · Ability to lift and carry athletic equipment and materials. · Ability to stand, walk, and move around frequently during practices and games. Working Conditions: · The position may require working outside in various weather conditions. · Some travel may be required for away games and tournaments.

Job.JobTitle: Coaching - HS Girls Varsity Head Track Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Cincinnati City School District is looking for a Track & Field Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Responsibilities: Oversee the entire Girls Track Program and serve as the Varsity Head Track Coach. Develop and train student-athletes to participate in competitive track. Develop and execute an effective training program. Monitor, motivate, supervise, and mentor student-athletes on and off the Track. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Instruct and guide student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Instruct and guild student-athletes to lead them to the formulation and enhancement of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, and self-confidence. Foster teamwork, sportsmanship, and skills development. Schedule a full season of contests including State-Tournament Track Meets. Work cooperatively and in a timely manner, with School Athletic Office and CPS District Athletics Department, in regard to all requests for information that might be periodically required. Check the status of academic eligibility for all prospective student-athletes. Follow District Athletic Department Policy as outlined in Coaches Handbook and meet all criteria as outlined in job description. Meet all requirements set forth by the State Board of Education, CPS, the Ohio High School Athletic Association (OHSAA), and The Ohio Department of Education (ODE) relative to the mandated Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) and ODE Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Recognize the need to be aware of and implement all applicable administrative policies set forth by the Board of Education, Superintendents of Schools, School Principals, Conference Leagues, OHSAA. Provide all applicable in-season training rules as well as any that may apply to out-of-season conduct to team members in writing prior to the start of the season. Follow appropriate due process procedures when violations occur. Model the behavior expected of our student-athletes including timeliness to games and practices. being positive and enthusiastic, being respectful, and practicing constructive criticism while upholding the disciplines of the program. Be appropriately dressed at all times. Maintain suitable sideline conduct, including the use of appropriate language, at all times. Accept and profit from constructive criticism and suggestions for improving coaching performance. Participate in clinics and other available programs to improve coaching performance. Participate in School Events (i.e., Awards Night Programs, Pep Assemblies, Athletic Boosters Meetings, etc.). Show physical vitality for the sport as well as use of good judgement and decision-making. Be competent in techniques of the sport so that student-athletes respond positively to directions given. Demonstrate the ability to adapt properly to changing game situations. Be well-versed and knowledgeable in matters pertaining to the sport and look at ways to constantly improve knowledge and coaching techniques. Be innovative in using new coaching techniques and ideas in addition to already proven methods of coaching. Ensure that team is well-prepared for opponents. Maintain accurate inventory of equipment and supplies. Skills/Essential Functions: Fundraising experience. Previous experience coaching track is required. Have a passion and dedication to motivate and inspire student-athletes. Ability to serve as a positive role model. Demonstrated knowledge of skills associated with this sport. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural environment. Training/Certifications: Ohio Secondary Teaching Certificate preferred. Successful participation in a similar activity in high school and/or college. Provide a satisfactory result on a County Police Records Check, State BCII Check, and FBI Fingerprint Background Check. Possess an Ohio Department of Education (ODE) Pupils Activity Permit (PAP). Provide proof of completion of Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification Contract Provisions: Type of Contract: Supplemental Annual Limited Salary: Per Schedule E of current C.F.T. Labor Agreement Reports To: Principal Application Procedure: Apply Online Selection Procedure: Interview

Twinsburg High School is looking for a Volleyball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
SUPPLEMENTAL COACHING POSITION (Varsity) QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. 2. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. 3. The coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 4. The coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her instructions and actions. Must Have : Pupil Activity Permit American Red Cross or American Heart Association training. First Aide Training Concussion Protocol Course REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who provides overall objectives and final evaluation in conjunction with the high school administration JOB GOAL: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self-discipline, self-confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. GENERAL: 1. The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not override the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health. 2. The position includes other unusual aspect such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments. 3. It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Twinsburg Board of Education. Schools' Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. 2. Has knowledge of existing system, state and league regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. STAFF RESPONSIBILITIES: 4. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. 5. Trains and informs staff, encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. 6. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation and, at season's end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. 7. Maintains discipline, and works to increase morale and cooperation. 8. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director/principal. ADMINISTRATIVE DUTIES: 9. Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. 10. Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. 11. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. 12. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. 13. Advises the athletic director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. STUDENT RESPONSIBILITIES : 14. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. 15. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. 16. By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. 17. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. 18. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies through collaboration with athletic trainer. 19. Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic trainer's office within 24 hours. 20. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. 21. Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational planning. FINANCE AND EQUIPMENT: 22. Participates in the budgeting function with the athletic director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations. 23. Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning same. 24. Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. 25. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorizes who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. 26. Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. 27. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. 28. Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. 29. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. PUBLIC RELATIONS: 30. Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for preseason meetings. 31. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program and promotes the sport outside the school through news media, community youth programs, or in any other feasible manner. 32. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. 33. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, booster club, parents, officials, volunteers and fans. 34. Presents information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and results.

At the same time, the assistant coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. GENERAL: 1. The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not override the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health. 2. The position includes other unusual aspects such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments. 3. It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment. QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Twinsburg City School District's Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. 2. Has knowledge of existing system, state and league regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. 3. Understands the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques of a soccer program. All coaches must have the following credentials before working with students: Pupil Activity Supervisor Permit CPR Credential Sudden Cardiac Arrest Certification Concussion in Sports Certification First Aid Certification Fundamentals of Coaching Certification BCI & FBI Background Checks See Attachment DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. 2. Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. 3. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. 4. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. 5. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. 6. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. 7. By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. 8. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. 9. Helps to initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. 10. Participates in the budgeting function with the head coach by establishing requirements for the next season. 11. Assists the head coach and properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. 12. Supervises athletes and makes sure that they are in authorized areas at the appropriate times. 13. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. 14. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. 15. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in other sports. 16. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director/principal.

Indian Hill High School is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
HEAD VOLLEYBALL COACH – POSITION POSTING Indian Hill Exempted Village School District To Apply: Email Brian Phelps , Athletic Director, with a resume and letter of interest . POSITION: Head Volleyball Coach STARTING DATE: Spring 2026 Season CATEGORY: Supplemental REPORTS TO: Athletic Director STATUS: FLSA Exempt; Section 3319.02 – ORC GENERAL DESCRIPTION Indian Hill High School is ready to welcome an inspiring leader to take the helm of a volleyball program bursting with potential, energy, and pride. Our student-athletes are competitive, committed, and hungry to grow — and they thrive in a district where excellence isn’t just encouraged, it’s expected. As the Head Volleyball Coach, you’ll shape a program that blends high-level skill development with culture-building, leadership, and the kind of team chemistry that makes something magical happen on and off the court. This is your chance to build a marquee volleyball experience in one of Ohio’s most admired school districts — a place where families are supportive, the athletic department is collaborative, and student-athletes buy in fully to the vision of a coach who leads with purpose. From practice strategy to game-day intensity, you’ll have the opportunity to elevate the entire program and create moments that athletes and fans will remember for years. If fueling competitive success, developing strong young leaders, and building a program rooted in heart, grit, and high expectations excites you — Indian Hill is ready for you to lead the charge. Indian Hill High School is a high-achieving Division II athletic program serving approximately 650 students in grades 9–12. Candidates must possess all required Ohio Department of Education coaching credentials and successfully complete FBI/BCI background checks. PREFERRED SKILLS • Prior volleyball coaching experience preferred • Understanding of systems, player development, and strategy • Ability to build positive team culture

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Tennis Coach - Cleveland HS - 25/26 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Cleveland HS is looking for a Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Posting Timeline This job is open until filled. Applications will be reviewed as received. Portland Public Schools reserves the right to make a hiring decision at any point during the posting period. Position Details DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions. DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265. Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities. The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics. All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings. Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference. All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records. Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00 * There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts. Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice. The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service. 504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660 Accessibility Statement Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process

Roswell Independent Schools is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Roswell Independent School District Job Description Job Title: ASSISTANT COACH Reports To: HEAD COACH/PRINCIPAL/DIRECTOR OF ATHLETICS/ACTIVITIES Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Has knowledge of existing system and State and NMAA regulations. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Roswell Independent School District Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation. Has knowledge of the existing system, state and league regulations and implements them consistently. Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperate within the school sports program. Recommends to the head coach budgetary items for next year in his area of the program. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches offices and authorizes who may enter. Is accountable to the head coach for all equipment. Collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned and arranges for issuing and storing of equipment and submits to the head coach an annual inventory and current records concerning this equipment. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of the specific equipment. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper usage. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Assists the head coach in scheduling, providing transportation to tournaments and special sporting events. Directs student managers and statisticians on respective games. Provides training rules and any other unique regulation of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Determines student discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary. By his presence at all practices, games and while traveling, provides assistance and guidance to and safeguards for each participant. Instructs team members as to changes in the rules and teaches fundamentals of the sport as outlined by the head coach. Assists the head coach in carrying out his responsibility. Attends all staff meetings and carries out scouting assignments as outlined by the head coach. Works within the basic framework and philosophy of the head coach of that sport. Arrives early enough before practice, contests and meetings to adequately prepare and remains long enough afterwards to help players with problems or to become involved in staff discussions. Never criticizes, admonishes or argues with head coach or any staff member within ears or eyes of players or parents. Helps in the planning and implementation of both in-season and out-or-season conditioning and weight programs as is possible. Attends most of the contests, when possible, or other teams in the program. Strives to improve skills by attending clinics and using resources made available by the head coach. Performs such other duties that are consistent with the nature of the position and that may be requested by the head coach. Maintain confidentiality with sensitive matters. Be flexible and able to prioritize tasks and maintain accurate and detailed records. Report to work on time and evening /weekend hours are applicable. Attempt to deescalate students/parents/staff; listening/visiting with them about their concerns prior to them meeting with administrators. May be required to perform other duties/functions as directed by supervisor. Page 1 of 2 Roswell Independent School District ASSISTANT COACH (CONT’D) Supervisory Responsibilities: Students in his charge. Qualifications: High School diploma or GED. Current NM Coaching licensure including CPR/AED Certification. Valid Driver’s license and Car Insurance. Ability to drive from site to site as needed.
... Find more information at: https://roswellnm.schoolspring.com/?jobid=5506098

Wappingers Central S.D. is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Wappingers Central School District is seeking coaching applicants for a variety of openings for the 2025-26 school year. Salary is based on the WCT Contractual coaching rates. Job.JobTitle: Head Girls Soccer Coach - Franklin HS - 26/27 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Portland Public Sch. is looking for a Soccer Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Details DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions. DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265. Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities. The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics.
All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings.
Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference. All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records. Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00 * There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts. Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice. The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service. 504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660 Accessibility Statement Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process

Job.JobDescription:
Bend-LaPine Sch. Dist. is looking for a Softball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application.
As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit, any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement.
Bend La Pine Schools Head Coaching Details
Applicant must submit:
1. Letter of application detailing experience. 2. Current Resume – listing experience related to this position and working with students. 3. A minimum of three letters of recommendation. 4. A minimum of three references with contact information.
General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Head Coach:
Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability academically and behaviorallyfor all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for program. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Understand and provide information regarding eligibility, tryout process, cut process, and all other team-related information to include lettering, team rules, etc. Be creative and involved in all fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with ALL stakeholders. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete.

Job.JobTitle: 2025-2026 SY - Athletic Coach - C-Team Softball (2 positions)
Job.JobDescription:
Gallup High School is looking for a Softball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Title: Athletic Coach  C Team Softball Work Location: Gallup High School Contract Length: Spring Sports Minimum Salary: $4,500.00 Maximum Salary: $4,500.00 *Internal applicants MUST attach a signed Permission to Coach Form to application* ATHLETIC COACHING LICENSE, 5-12 Must hold or have the ability to obtain an Athletic Coaching License ( https://webnew.ped.state.nm.us/bureaus/licensure/how-to-apply/athletic-coach-5-12/ ) from the New Mexico Public Education Department as well as completing the Fundamentals of Coaching online exam offered through the New Mexico Activities Association. (www.nmact.org) QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. 2. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. (Major sports) 3. The head coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 4. The head coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her instructions and actions. REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who provides overall objectives and final evaluation in conjunction with the high school principals. WORK ENVIRONMENT: On-site attendance is mandatory for this position. SUPERVISES: In several instances, the head coach must advise, coordinate and support a staff of high school assistant coaches and middle school coaches in conjunction with the athletic director and respective principal. JOB GOAL: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self-discipline, self-confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. Work with students interested in sports, provide necessary practice support and advice for a team to be successful against opposing teams in a sports season. Teach and enforce rules and regulations. Involves work on evenings and weekends.

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Football Head Coach - 8th Grade
Job.JobDescription:
Bunsold Middle School is looking for a Football Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
REPORTS TO : Middle School Principal and Athletic Director EMPLOYMENT STATUS: Temporary, part-time POSITION SUMMARY : Coach middle school grade level team(s), supervise a grade level middle school football program and coordinate the middle school program between grade levels and with high school program. ESSENTIAL FUNCTION: Organize and coach all practice sessions. Coach all scheduled games. Organize and coordinate practice schedules of all football teams at that grade level. Assure that students are supervised continuously and require students to behave appropriately. Supervise the distribution, collection and inventory of all equipment. Prepare written inventory of all equipment prior to start of season and at conclusion of season. Inspect all equipment and be responsible for seeing that equipment is maintained and safe for student use. Prepare equipment for reconditioning. Coordinate and supervise fitting of students for equipment. Prepare a written practice schedule and distribute weekly to students/parents, Middle School Athletic Director and Middle School Administrators. Collect, maintain, and distribute to appropriate personnel all emergency medical forms and medical information about students. Conduct parent meeting prior to start of season. Prepare budget for MMS football program that is submitted to MMS Athletic Director. Prepare written budget requests for MMS athletic director that will be forwarded to Quarterback Club. Comply with the academic eligibility requirements of the Ohio High School Athletic Association, the Marysville School District and the Marysville Middle School. Inform students and their parents weekly of any eligibility problems or if student is ineligible Organize and conduct award program. Observe all safety and health regulations of the school district. Maintain medical kits. Prepare and organize all necessary records and forms. Complete paperwork as directed by MMS athletic director and/or building administrators. Serve as a role model for student athletes in the promotion and practice of sportsmanship, ethical principles and responsible behavior. Communicate effectively and work cooperatively with students, staff, parents and community members. Prepare written announcements regarding practices, games, etc. for MMS announcements. Prepare written summaries and information concerning MMS football games, etc. for local newspaper. Inspect MMS game and practice facilities and report to MMS Athletic Director any concerns, etc. concerning facilities. Uphold the rules and guidelines of the OHSAA, Board of Education, Athletic Department and the MMS Student Handbook. Coordinate the MMS Football program with MHS Football program. Communicate with MHS Head Varsity Coach and MHS Football assistants about skills to be taught, etc. at this level. Maintain the locker room facilities. Organize and supervise the physical conditioning program prior to start of season. Provide written information to students and MMS Athletic Director about such programs prior to end of previous school year. Communicate any violations of policies or improper conduct of coaches to MMS Athletic Director in a prompt and timely manner. Supervise other MMS Football coaches and provide written input to MMS Athletic Director for the purpose of evaluating these coaches. Communicate regularly and frequently with the other MMS Head Football Coach in order to coordinate the total MMS Football program. OTHER DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Other duties may be assigned by the Middle School Athletic Director, Middle School Principal or High School Head Football Coach. QUALIFICATIONS: Pupil Activity Supervisor Validation. Ohio teaching certificate/license preferred. CPR certification with mandatory annual review. REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES : Ability to teach and evaluate the skills of the sport. Football experience and/or knowledge of football. Ability to effectively supervise high school athletes and work with them in a positive manner. Ability to work cooperatively with adults. Ability to meet the practice and contest time requirements specified by the Athletic Director or Principal. EQUIPMENT OPERATED : Blocking and tackling sleds. Audiovisual equipment. WORKING CONDITIONS: Potential exposure to blood or other bodily fluids. Regular exposure to hazardous athletic equipment. Close proximity to people running. Potential exposure to unruly people. Exposure to dust, pollen and inclement weather conditions. NOTE: The above lists may not be ranked in order of importance. CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT: Length of Employment: Contract runs concurrent with school year; Football season will run approximately August through November Salary: Supplemental pay, step one or appropriate experience step level Fringe Benefits: None ADDITIONAL INFORMATION: Please submit a letter of interest and resume to the online application. Please do not send materials through U.S. mail or by e-mail, all documents should be uploaded to your application. If your qualifications meet our needs we will contact you for further information and a possible interview. The Marysville Exempted Village School District is an EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER and is in compliance with SECTION 504 OF THE REHABILITATION ACT OF 1973. Posted Internally: January 20, 2026

Job.JobTitle: Head Cheerleading Coach - Immediate Opening or Fall 2026
Job.JobDescription:
Indian Hill High School is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
HEAD CHEERLEADING COACH – POSITION POSTING Indian Hill Exempted Village School District POSITION: Head Cheerleading Coach STARTING DATE: IMMEDIATE, SPRING 2026 or FALL 2027 CATEGORY: Supplemental REPORTS TO: Athletic Director STATUS: FLSA Exempt; Section 3319.02 – ORC TERMS: If interested, contact: Brian Phelps , Athletic Director GENERAL DESCRIPTION Indian Hill High School is home to one of Ohio’s most vibrant, high-performing school communities — a place where excellence is the expectation and spirit runs deep. As the Head Cheerleading Coach, you’ll lead a program that energizes our athletes, ignites our crowds, and helps shape the culture of pride across our campus. Our cheerleaders are enthusiastic, hardworking, and eager to grow, and they thrive under strong leadership that blends skill development, teamwork, and tradition. This role offers the opportunity to build a dynamic, student-centered cheer program that inspires school spirit at games, competitions, and community events. You’ll coach in a district known statewide for its commitment to student success, supportive families, and a collaborative athletic department that values innovation and heart. If you’re passionate about developing athletes, cultivating leadership, and creating unforgettable experiences for students and fans alike — Indian Hill is the place to make your mark. Indian Hill High School is a high-achieving Division II athletic program serving approximately 650 students in grades 9–12. The district emphasizes excellence, character, and student development. Candidates must possess all required Ohio Department of Education coaching credentials and successfully complete FBI/BCI background checks. PREFERRED SKILLS • Previous head coaching or assistant coaching experience in cheerleading preferred • Experience in choreography, stunting safety, and game-day leadership • Ability to develop strong culture, spirit, and team cohesion

Job.JobDescription:
Pennridge is looking for a Soccer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Details Title: Head Coach - Freshman Soccer Reports to: Athletic Director Location: High School Available: ASAP Terms of Employment: Stipend in accordance with EDR Contract as stated in the PEA Collective Bargaining Agreement. Description: Pennridge School District is seeking a dedicated, experienced, and student-centered individual to serve as the Head Coach for our freshman soccer program. This position plays a critical role in developing student-athletes both on and off the field, fostering a culture of excellence, sportsmanship, and teamwork. Key Responsibilities: Lead and manage all aspects of the freshman soccer program at the high school level. Plan, organize, and supervise practices and competitions. Ensure the physical and emotional safety of all student-athletes. Promote academic achievement, character development, and personal responsibility. Supervise assistant coaches and collaborate with athletic department staff. Maintain and inventory equipment and uniforms. Represent the program positively within the school and the broader community. Comply with school, district, league, and state athletic association policies and regulations. Minimum Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent (Bachelor’s degree preferred). Prior coaching experience, preferably at the high school level. Knowledge of rules, strategies, and techniques of the sport. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to build positive relationships with students, parents, and staff. Preferred Qualifications: Valid teaching certificate (if applicable or required by district). CPR/First Aid certification. NFHS coaching certification or willingness to obtain. Experience managing budgets, schedules, and program development. Pre-Employment Paperwork Requirements : Physical and Clearances must be dated within the last 12-months. TB must be dated within the last 90-days. PA State Police Clearance (Act 34) Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151) FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) School Personnel Health Form (Physical and TB test) PDE 6004 Form (Act 24) Sexual Misconduct/Abuse Disclosure Release Form(s) (Act 168) Procedure: Interested candidates must submit and application online. EOE

Job.JobTitle: Boys Tennis Assistant Coach at Hilton Head Island High School
Job.JobDescription:
JobID: 17470 is looking for a Tennis Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: Prepares each participating student athlete for competition in sports activities, and helps them to achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Prioritize athletics as a supplemental program to support the academic mission of the school. Carry out the aims and objectives of the sports program as outlined by the Athletic Director and school district administration. Instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, sportsmanship, cooperation, strategy and playing tactics, and physical conditioning necessary to realize individual and team success. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. Maintain required attendance forms, insurance records, and other related documents. Assist in oversight of safety conditions in the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times student athletes are present. Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, uniforms, etc. as appropriate. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Athletic Director, Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. Knowledge and/or background in the assigned sport. Teaching certificate and experience preferred. Must be able to pass criminal background check. Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE : Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. Must have the ability to exercise good judgment, organize tasks, follow written and verbal directions, and communicate effectively. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. REPORTS TO: Athletic Director DAYS PER YEAR: To be Determined SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Paid on the Stipend Schedule EMPLOYEE GROUP: Part-Time Temporary APPLICATION PROCEDURES: Must apply online by completing the appropriate application and adding this specific job to your online application. FLSA STATUS: Non-Exempt EMPLOYEE'S ACKNOWLEDGEMENT STATEMENT: 1. I have reviewed the above position description and understand its contents. 2. I am aware that my position description may be revised or updated at any time and once notified of changes, I remain responsible for knowledge of its contents. 3. I hereby certify that I possess the physical and mental ability to fulfill the essential functions of the above position with or without reasonable accommodation(s). If I require accommodation(s) in order to fulfill any or all of these functions, I agree to provide information to the District regarding the requested accommodation(s). Rock Hill is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. Satisfactory background report Preferred: Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.) Start Date: Determined by Human Resources PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations. DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs. EEOC STATEMENT: Rock Hill Schools provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Job.JobDescription:
Providence Public Sch. is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Under direction of the school principal, Head Coach and the Athletic Director/or appropriate supervisor/Administrator, Individuals will be available three hours each day participate at games, and scout opponents whenever assigned to this task by the Head Coach. This position is contingent upon the following: Preference will be given to internal/school based staff and co-op school staff. Successful recruitment of team members and ability to field a team for practice and game conditions. You must complete the new hire compliance training prior to your first day of employment Bilingual Preferred Selected candidate will be required to pass a pre-employment criminal history background check. Selected candidates cannot begin work until passing their background check. Proper physical conditioning related to the sport during practice sessions. Successful completion of the interscholastic season Salary Rate Range: 8% of Teacher Salary Scale: Track, Outdoor Track, Boys/Girls Basketball, Softball, Soccer, Softball, Volleyball 10% of Teacher Salary Scale: Football EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: Must be certified in accordance with the new RIIL Coaching Permit Requirements DESCRIPTION OF DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES: Follow instructions issued by the Head Coach. Assist in the issuing, maintaining, and collecting of equipment. Perform duties in accordance with the Providence School Department Athletic Handbook. Teach fundamentals and skills. Teach rules and regulations. Display good sportsmanship at all times. Supervise players at all times. Assume responsibility for related duties that may be assigned by the Head Coach. EEO Statement Providence Public Schools is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that is actively seeking to increase minority recruitment among its administrators and teaching staff. PSD does not discriminate on the basis of race, age, sex, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, color, disability or veteran status. All programs are open to all individuals, regardless of their race, age, sex, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, color, disability or veteran status. Notice of E-Verify Participation and Right to Work Providence Public School District participates in E-Verify . Federal law requires all participating employers to verify the identity and employment eligibility of all persons hired to work in the United States. Please review the following posters for more information: Notice of E-Verify Participation (English/Spanish) IER Right to Work Poster (English/Spanish)
